title: DocumentStockMaterialLibrary Object
description: DocumentStockMaterialLibrary provides access to stock materials used by the document.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: item
methods.description: Get StockMaterial by index in DocumentStockMaterialLibrary.

methods.name: setupsByStockMaterial
methods.description: Returns all setups that use the given StockMaterial. The StockMaterial must exist in the document StockMaterial library. Raises an error if the StockMaterial is not in the document.

methods.name: update
methods.description: Update the given StockMaterial in the document StockMaterial library. The update applies all changes to the stockMaterial in the document stockMaterial library and therefore on all setups that use the stockMaterial. Will error if the stockMaterial does not exist in the document stockMaterial library.

properties.name: count
properties.description: The number of StockMaterial in the DocumentStockMaterialLibrary.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

classType.title: DocumentStockMaterialLibrary.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

item.title: DocumentStockMaterialLibrary.item Method
item.description: Get StockMaterial by index in DocumentStockMaterialLibrary.
item.return_description: Returns the StockMaterial in the DocumentStockMaterialLibrary by given index.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: Index of the StockMaterial in the DocumentStockMaterialLibrary.

setupsByStockMaterial.title: DocumentStockMaterialLibrary.setupsByStockMaterial Method
setupsByStockMaterial.description: Returns all setups that use the given StockMaterial. The StockMaterial must exist in the document StockMaterial library. Raises an error if the StockMaterial is not in the document.
setupsByStockMaterial.return_description: Returns setups using a specific StockMaterial.

parameters.name: stockMaterial
parameters.description: The StockMaterial to search for setups. The StockMaterial must exist in the document.

update.title: DocumentStockMaterialLibrary.update Method
update.description: Update the given StockMaterial in the document StockMaterial library. The update applies all changes to the stockMaterial in the document stockMaterial library and therefore on all setups that use the stockMaterial. Will error if the stockMaterial does not exist in the document stockMaterial library.
update.return_description: Returns true if the update was successful.

parameters.name: stockMaterial
parameters.description: The StockMaterial that should be updated.

count.title: DocumentStockMaterialLibrary.count Property
count.description: The number of StockMaterial in the DocumentStockMaterialLibrary.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = documentStockMaterialLibrary_var.count

isValid.title: DocumentStockMaterialLibrary.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = documentStockMaterialLibrary_var.isValid

objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:

propertyValue = documentStockMaterialLibrary_var.objectType
